These include managing tight schedules, coordinating childcare arrangements, and dealing with longstanding issues regarding support in the workplace. This information is being shared by Mediaplanet.

data.title: Success in achieving health goals is possible with diligence and follow-through. Approach goals with determination.
data.description: Achieving your health goals is just the beginning; what really matters is what you do next. Success hinges on your determination and commitment to see it through. If you approach your goals with diligence and drive, you are on the right path to achieving them. Remember, health goals are attainable if you have the right mindset and approach.

data.title: Stock Yards Bancorp appoints David L. Hardy to the Boards of Directors for both the Company and Bank, effective Oct. 21, 2025 in KY. (Numbers: 1 person, Oct. 21, 2025)
data.description: Louisville, Kentucky-based Stock Yards Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: SYBT) revealed that David L. Hardy will join the Boards of Directors for both the Company and the Bank starting from October 21, 2025. The parent company of Stock Yards Bank & Trust Company operates in Louisville, central, eastern, and northern Kentucky, along with the Indianapolis, Indiana, and Cincinnati, Ohio metropolitan areas. Hardy's appointment signifies a key addition to the governance structure of both entities.

data.title: BULGOLD reports Q2 financial results: $114,537 net loss for 3 months, $152,428 for 6 months, $255,401 cash on hand.
data.description: In Toronto on August 22, 2025, BULGOLD Inc. (TSXV: ZLTO) has announced the submission of its unaudited condensed interim financial statements and MD&A for the quarter ended June 30, 2025. During this period, the company experienced a net comprehensive loss of $114,537 ($0.00 per share) for the 3 months, and $152,428 ($0.01 per share) for the first 6 months of the year. As of June 30, 2025, BULGOLD held $255,401 in cash (and cash equivalents).

data.title: Open Banking Solutions Market set to reach $11.7 billion by 2028, with Plaid, Envestnet, and Tink leading. Growth driven by consumer preference for mobile banking apps, web portals, and transparency in financial activities. Opportunity lies in open-banking APIs and collaboration among key players.
data.description: The Open Banking Solutions Market is projected to expand from USD 5.5 billion in 2023 to USD 11.7 billion by 2028, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 16.0% during the forecast period. Factors driving this growth include consumers' increasing preference for mobile apps for banking transactions, web portals serving as comprehensive platforms for digital banking, and the convenience of tracking financial activities online. Key market players include Plaid (US), Envestnet (US), Tink (Sweden), Finicity (US), and more. The market is being driven by increased consumer demand for cloud-based solutions, compliance requirements of new data laws, and the need for greater visibility in financial transactions. Opportunities lie in the adoption of open-banking APIs, demand for innovative products, and collaboration among key industry players.

data.title: DeFi Technologies starts Normal Course Issuer Bid; traded on Nasdaq: DEFT, CBOE CA: DEFI, GR: R9B - bridging traditional capital markets and DeFi.
data.description: In a recent announcement on August 22, 2025, DeFi Technologies Inc. (Nasdaq: DEFT, CBOE CA: DEFI, GR: R9B) shared that they are a financial technology company focused on connecting traditional capital markets with decentralized finance ("DeFi").

data.title: Global OTC drugs market expected to reach USD 242.94 billion by 2034, up from USD 135.25 billion in 2025, with a 6.72% CAGR growth. Major markets: North America - USD 39.77 billion in 2024, Europe - USD 27.71 billion in 2024, Asia Pacific - USD 52.51 billion in 2024.
data.description: The global over the counter (OTC) drugs market is projected to be worth over USD 242.94 billion by 2034, increasing from USD 135.25 billion in 2025 with a strong CAGR of 6.72% from 2025 to 2034. Factors contributing to this growth include the expansion of e-commerce, rising popularity of self-medication, and increasing healthcare expenditure. OTC drugs are medications sold without a prescription, used to treat minor conditions like colds, headaches, and allergies. They are available in various formats like tablets and ointments and are sold in convenience stores, pharmacies, and online. In 2024, the North America market exceeded USD 39.77 billion, Europe accounted for USD 27.71 billion, and Asia Pacific reached USD 52.51 billion. The oral segment held a market share of 56.66% in 2024, while the parenteral segment had a 20.24% market share. Key products contributing to market size include vitamin and dietary supplements, cough and cold products, analgesics, and gastrointestinal products.

data.title: AGF Investments declares August 2025 cash distributions for AGF Enhanced U.S. Equity Income Fund, AGF Total Return Bond Fund, and AGF Systematic Global Infrastructure ETF. Record date: August 29, 2025; payout on September 5, 2025. TSX:AGF.B.
data.description: On August 22, 2025, AGF Investments Inc. shared updates regarding the August 2025 cash distributions for a few of its funds. The AGF Enhanced U.S. Equity Income Fund, AGF Total Return Bond Fund, and AGF Systematic Global Infrastructure ETF, all of which offer monthly distributions, were specifically highlighted. Cash distributions will be made to unitholders of record on August 29, 2025, with the payments scheduled for September 5, 2025. These distributions are of significance to investors in TSX-listed AGF Investments, with the announcement aimed at providing clarity and transparency regarding the upcoming payments for the mentioned funds.

data.title: Americas Gold and Silver finds high-grade 149 Vein extension - 24,913 g/t Silver and 16.9% Copper; share consolidation completed.
data.description: Americas Gold and Silver Corporation, a prominent North American precious metals producer trading under the symbols TSX: USA and NYSE American: USAS, has released positive exploration findings at the Galena Complex. Interestingly, these results reveal the discovery of a high-grade upper extension of the 149 Vein. In addition to this exciting news, the Company has successfully implemented a share consolidation as previously disclosed. Following this consolidation, trading is anticipated to occur on a post-consolidation basis.
